Gas Robot Variant

Gas Robot is an advanced variant of the platform, featuring expanded capabilities for autonomous navigation, environmental perception, computer vision, and gas detection.

Gas Robot Specific

  • Gas Robot Setup
    • Overview
    • Prerequisites
    • Installation
    • Hardware Requirements
    • Core Subsystems
    • Configuration
    • Environment Variables
    • Building and Testing
    • Troubleshooting
    • Next Steps
  • Gas Robot Subsystems
    • System Flow
    • Module Roles
    • Design Intent
    • Related Documentation

Getting Started with Gas Robot

See the Getting Started for initial setup, then proceed with Gas Robot Setup for Gas Robot-specific configuration.

Core Subsystems

Gas Robot includes advanced subsystems:

  • Communication - Multi-layer communication architecture

  • Hardware Interfaces - Sensor and actuator drivers

  • Perception - Environmental sensor fusion

  • State Estimation - Accurate pose tracking

  • Navigation - Autonomous path planning and navigation

  • Mapping - SLAM and environmental mapping

  • CV Pipeline - Computer vision processing

Shared Platform & Firmware

Gas Robot shares the core platform with Wall-E:

  • Architecture

  • Communication Model

  • ESP32 Controller

Development & Deployment

For development workflows, see Workflow and Commands.

For deployment information, see WiFi Provisioning.
